title: Fix silent fallback to dummy results in results_parser.py
description: results_parser.py falls back to dummy/fabricated result data when FRD parsing fails, with no error raised and no warning surfaced to the engineer. An engineer can receive a plausible-looking displacement/stress result that is completely made up. This must raise ResultProcessingError instead of silently returning fake data.

title: Equation node distance 5m > 1m in test_equation_node_distances_structure
description: tests/integration/test_connection_equations.py::TestConnectionEquations::test_equation_node_distances_structure fails: 'Max equation node distance 5.00m > 1.0m — nodes are too far apart (bad element-to-member mapping?)'. The EQUATION constraints generated in the CalculiX INP file reference nodes that are 5m apart instead of adjacent nodes at the same connection point. This indicates element-to-member mapping is assigning wrong nodes to connection equations. Needs investigation of which model is used and why equation node pairs are so far apart.

title: check_completion_status treats inconclusive output as failure, breaking CalculiX integration
description: OutputParser.check_completion_status() returns (False, 'Unable to determine if operation completed successfully') when no success pattern is found in CalculiX output. This causes AnalysisError to be raised even when CalculiX ran successfully but its output doesn't match any of the four hardcoded SUCCESS_PATTERNS ('ANALYSIS COMPLETED', 'ANALYSIS TERMINATED SUCCESSFULLY', 'SOLUTION CONVERGED', 'STEP COMPLETED'). All integration and validation tests that call CalculiX fail with this error. The fix is to either match CalculiX's actual output strings, or treat inconclusive as success (since return code 0 already indicates success). Affects: test_end_to_end.py, test_building_analysis.py, test_results_json.py, test_cantilever_benchmark.py, test_simply_supported_benchmark.py, test_buckling_benchmark.py.

title: Fix geometry fallback to hardcoded [0,0,0]-[10,0,0] in entity_identifier.py
description: When member geometry cannot be parsed from IFC, entity_identifier.py silently substitutes a hardcoded 10m line at the origin. The model then meshes and analyses with completely wrong geometry, producing wrong results with no warning. Should raise ModelExtractionError with the IFC GUID of the failing member.

title: Add CalculiX execution timeout
description: calculix_runner.py has no timeout on the CalculiX subprocess. A diverging analysis or infinite loop hangs the tool indefinitely with no feedback to the engineer. Should add a configurable timeout (default e.g. 300s) via AnalysisConfig, kill the process on expiry, and raise AnalysisError with a clear message.

title: Add pre-analysis model validation
description: Before meshing, the tool should validate that the extracted domain model is well-conditioned: all members have material and section properties, all members are connected to at least one boundary condition or other member (no floating islands), all loads are attached to members, no duplicate member IDs. Currently a disconnected structure is sent to CalculiX and fails obscurely.

title: Fix convergence detection logic bug in output_parser.py
description: Line 129 of output_parser.py uses 'and' instead of 'or': 'if not completed and "ANALYSIS INTERRUPTED" in output_text'. This means a run that has no completion marker AND no INTERRUPTED marker is treated as converged. Should be 'or', so any missing-completion case triggers a non-convergence warning.

title: Fix hardcoded material fallback: missing material data should error, not default to steel
description: When an IFC member has no material or incomplete mechanical properties, properties_extractor.py silently substitutes steel defaults (E=210 GPa, rho=7850 kg/m3). A concrete slab or timber beam then analyses with completely wrong material properties. Should warn loudly (or error) and require explicit material data, not guess.

title: Fix mesh size default inconsistency: CLI help says 1.0 m, code defaults to 0.1 m
description: The --mesh-size option in commands.py advertises a default of 1.0 m in the help text, but the actual default in the code is 0.1 m. This is confusing and produces unexpectedly fine (slow) meshes without the engineer requesting them. Pick one value, document it consistently, and use it everywhere.

title: Fix multiple load cases: each IfcStructuralLoadCase should map to a separate CalculiX STEP
description: Models with multiple IfcStructuralLoadCase entries (Dead, Live, Wind, Earthquake etc.) currently only get a subset of cases written to the INP. Each load case should produce a separate *STEP in the CalculiX input file so all cases are analysed and their results are tagged by case name. This affects correctness for any real multi-case building model.

title: Improve self-weight / gravity load handling
description: Models without explicit IfcStructuralLoadCase entries rely entirely on self-weight, but the --gravity flag must be passed manually. The library has no way to auto-detect that gravity is the intended load. Consider smarter defaults or detection.

title: Validate linear buckling analysis against published benchmarks
description: The linear_buckling analysis type produces two CalculiX steps (static pre-stress + perturbation buckle) and parses eigenvalue multipliers from the .dat file, but results have not been validated against any published benchmark. Add a validation test.

title: Buckling benchmark finds no eigenvalues in .dat file
description: test_buckling_benchmark.py::TestDirectCalculixBucklingValidation tests fail: 'No buckling eigenvalues found in result files'. The results_parser.py may not parse eigenvalues from CalculiX .dat output, or the *BUCKLE step output format doesn't match what the parser expects. Needs investigation of the .dat file contents and results_parser.py's eigenvalue parsing logic.

title: portal_01 shows 0 displacement: U1 element DLOAD not applied
description: test_results_json.py::TestPortal01ResultsJson::test_displacement_plausible fails because portal_01 has a distributed load on a U1 (user element) beam, but boundary_condition_handling.py explicitly skips DLOAD for U1 elements ('Skipping DLOAD for MEMBER_M3 — U1 elements do not support *DLOAD'). With no loads applied, CalculiX produces 0 displacement. The test asserts 0 < max_disp < 0.1. Fix: either apply the load via CLOAD at nodes (convert distributed load to equivalent nodal loads for U1 elements), or change portal_01.ifc to not use U1 sections.

title: Extractor raises ModelExtractionError for invalid type but test expects ValueError
description: tests/integration/test_extraction_pipeline.py::TestExtractionPipeline::test_extractor_error_handling passes an integer (123) to Extractor() and expects ValueError. But extractor.py:69 raises ModelExtractionError instead. Fix: either change extractor.py to raise ValueError for invalid input types, or update the test to expect ModelExtractionError.

title: calculix_timeout=600 added to analyze_ifc/run_enhanced_analysis without updating tests
description: run_enhanced_analysis() and analyze_ifc() now pass calculix_timeout=600 in their call signatures, but tests in test_commands.py (TestEnhancedCLICommands.test_analyze_successful, test_analyze_custom_params) and test_structural_analysis.py (test_run_enhanced_analysis_backward_compatibility) use assert_called_once_with() without the calculix_timeout kwarg, so they fail. Fix: update the mock assertions in those tests to include calculix_timeout=600.

title: Write missing unit tests for recent changes
description: Several recent bug fixes and features lack unit test coverage: _validate_model(), loads_extractor GRAVITY group fix, LoadConfiguration averaging, L/T/C section extractors, non-SI unit warning, CLI --timeout.
close_reason: 59 new unit tests written and passing — all 7 coverage gaps addressed

title: Fix IfcStructuralLoadConfiguration: only first load value is used
description: loads_extractor.py line 675-679 reads IfcStructuralLoadConfiguration but only uses the first value from the Values array. Multi-point load distributions (e.g. trapezoidal line loads) are silently truncated to a point load. Should either implement proper interpolated distribution or raise a clear not-implemented warning.

title: Document and stabilise result JSON schema for downstream tools
description: The results JSON written by run_enhanced_analysis() has no documented schema. Keys, units, and structure can change between versions without warning, breaking downstream tools that process the results. Define a versioned schema (e.g. results_v1.json), document it, and add a schema version key to the output. Units should be explicitly stated (SI: metres, Newtons, Pascals).

title: Consolidate duplicate error/convergence detection logic
description: OutputParser (output_parser.py) and ErrorDetector (error_handling.py) independently define the same ~40 CalculiX error patterns and convergence checks. Any fix or new pattern must be applied in two places. Consolidate into a single implementation — OutputParser should delegate to ErrorDetector, or merge into one class.

title: Validate IFC project units and warn on non-SI input
description: The extractor applies unit scale factors but never validates that they are reasonable. A model authored in imperial units (feet, kips) with a missing unit declaration will produce silently wrong results. Should check extracted unit scales against expected SI ranges and warn/error if they look wrong (e.g. length_scale not in [0.001, 1000]).

title: Support I-section beam profiles natively
description: Only rectangular, circular, pipe (hollow circle), and box (hollow rectangle) cross-sections are supported for CalculiX B31 beam elements. I-sections are currently approximated as equivalent rectangles preserving area and second moment of area. Proper CalculiX B31 I-section support should be added.

title: Support partial connection end-releases
description: Connection end-releases read from IfcRelConnectsStructuralMember.AppliedCondition are modelled as full pins (all three rotational DOFs released). Partial releases — where only one member or one rotation axis is released — are not yet supported.
close_reason: Per-member per-axis release tracking implemented; writer emits partial EQUATION coupling; 29 new tests

title: Fix overlapping members losing physical group during mesh fragmentation
description: Members with identical or overlapping geometry may lose their physical group assignment during fragment(). Their sections are skipped with a warning; a fallback assigns the nearest elements by spatial proximity. Need a robust fix so physical groups survive fragmentation.
close_reason: Exact-overlap members now share a combined physical group ('A||B'). Writer parses pipe-separated names and assigns elements to all named members. Partial-overlap fallback preserved.

title: Fix connection geometry: use IFC topology instead of proximity search
description: Currently connections are resolved by geometric proximity (0.5 m tolerance) rather than using the IfcRelConnectsStructuralMember relationship topology defined in the IFC file. This can cause incorrect connectivity for closely spaced but unconnected members.
close_reason: Replaced 0.5m proximity search with endpoint-targeting: uses domain model geometry to find member endpoint closest to connection position, then matches mesh node within 10mm. Falls back to 0.5m for members without geometry.

title: slab_01 has no loads: displacement envelope always empty
description: test_results_json.py::TestSlab01ResultsJson::test_member_envelope_present fails because slab_01.ifc has no IfcStructuralLoadCase entries. CalculiX runs with no loads → 0 displacement → results.json has no displacement envelope data. Either the model needs gravity loads added (--gravity flag), or the test needs to pass --gravity, or slab_01.ifc needs a load case added.

title: Module-level sys.modules stubs break test_gmsh_utils.py collection (fixed) and other tests
description: Seven test files stub ifc_structural_mechanics.meshing.gmsh_utils at module level using 'if not in sys.modules: _stub(...)'. Since tests/unit/api/ sorts before tests/unit/meshing/ alphabetically, test_validate_model.py's stub poisons sys.modules before test_gmsh_utils.py is collected, causing ImportError for GmshExecutableRunner. Partially fixed in test_gmsh_utils.py by adding sys.modules.pop() before the import (same pattern as test_support_point_seeding.py for gmsh_geometry). Root problem: all these module-level stubs are fragile and test-order-dependent. Long-term fix: move stubs into conftest.py fixtures or autouse fixtures scoped to the test session, so ordering doesn't matter. Also affects test_support_point_seeding.py in full-suite runs.

title: Standardise exception handling: use custom exception hierarchy consistently
description: The codebase has a custom exception hierarchy in utils/error_handling.py (ModelExtractionError, MeshingError, AnalysisError, ResultProcessingError) but it is used in only ~30 of ~177 raise sites. The remaining 147 raises use plain ValueError or RuntimeError, meaning callers cannot distinguish mesh failures from parse failures or IFC extraction errors by catching specific types. Additionally, the error_context() context manager in error_handling.py has zero callers despite being written to wrap exceptions with pipeline context. The fix is to audit all raise sites in ifc/, meshing/, and analysis/ and replace plain exceptions with the appropriate custom type, and wire error_context() into the key pipeline stages.

title: Extract MeshMapper and SectionWriter from UnifiedCalculixWriter
description: unified_calculix_writer.py is 2043 lines with 60+ methods handling too many concerns: mesh I/O, three element-to-member mapping strategies, section writing, material writing, connection EQUATION writing, U1 user-element tracking, gravity load pre-computation, and mechanism detection. The element-to-member mapping section (physical groups → spatial → naive fallback) is the most fragile part and has required the most bug fixes (zvo, 4eu, sjk). Extracting a MeshMapper class (owns the three mapping strategies and their selection logic) and a SectionWriter class (owns beam/shell section INP generation) would make each concern independently testable and easier to navigate. The orchestration and file-writing coordination stays in UnifiedCalculixWriter.

title: Fix mixed beam+shell instability: rotational zero-energy modes under lateral load
description: When B31 beam elements and shell elements are connected only via DOF 1-3 EQUATION constraints, and column bases are pinned (DOF 1-3 only from IfcBoundaryNodeCondition), the structure has rotational zero-energy modes under lateral load. building_01 demonstrates this: lateral forces on a pinned-base frame with no moment-resisting connections produce a near-mechanism. At minimum, the library should detect and warn about this condition rather than silently producing wrong results. The fix may involve upgrading shared beam+shell node connections to DOF 1-6 EQUATION constraints where appropriate.

title: Fix intermediate support positions being silently omitted
description: If a support connection is at an intermediate point on a beam (not at a beam endpoint), Gmsh may not place a mesh node at that exact location, causing the support to be silently omitted. The grid_of_beams example demonstrates this.

title: Add missing type annotations to private methods in meshing layer
description: 17+ private methods in unified_calculix_writer.py and gmsh_geometry.py lack parameter or return type annotations, reducing IDE support and making mypy unable to catch type errors in the most complex parts of the codebase. Key gaps: _extract_cell_blocks, _assign_elements_spatially, _distribute_elements_to_members, _write_list_in_chunks, _write_beam_section_for_set, _write_beam_section_general, _write_point_connection, _find_connection_nodes_at_location (all in unified_calculix_writer.py); _ensure_gmsh_initialized, _create_curve_geometry, _create_surface_geometry, apply_mesh_size, _create_rectangle_in_plane (in gmsh_geometry.py). At minimum, return types should be added; parameter types should follow.

title: Fix direct mutation of config._config dict in structural_analysis.py
description: api/structural_analysis.py lines 380 and 406 mutate the internal _config dict of AnalysisConfig directly (config._config['analysis_type'] = ..., config._config['solver_params'] = ...) instead of using setter methods. This bypasses any validation logic in the setter path and breaks encapsulation. The fix is to add setter methods to AnalysisConfig (or use keyword arguments at construction time) and remove the direct dict access.

title: Remove or document unused public methods in extraction and analysis layers
description: 16 public methods exist in src/ with no callers: extraction-layer APIs (extract_all_members, extract_all_connections, extract_connection_by_id, get_warnings, get_pset_property, get_pset_properties) that predate the unified Extractor.extract_model() approach; output_parser methods (classify_error_severity, map_error_to_entity, generate_error_summary) that appear superseded by ErrorDetector but retained without explanation; and calculix_runner getters (get_result_files, set_mapper, get_mapper) that are never called. Each should either be deleted (if truly dead), documented with a note explaining why it exists, or moved to a separate optional API surface.
